title: Joint reconstruction-canonicalization trunk: a from-scratch encoder that must repaint the clean daytime crop, trained alongside localization every step
hypothesis: Backbone is off-limits this round (mnv3s_features8.pt / mobilenet_v3_small banned), and the last seven rounds (36-42) were jointly refuted while leaving one thing unquestioned at a time: 36/37/39/40 churned the loss/decode/field parameterization around the SAME ImageNet-classification trunk, and 41/42 finally replaced that trunk with a from-scratch one but taught it via NT-Xent instance-discrimination contrastive pretraining -- and still lost (846.42 m vs champion 743.07 m). Exp 42's own pre-registered falsification fork (a) named the exact redirect for a flat/worse contrastive result: 'a reconstruction-style auxiliary (library L1) instead of contrastive' -- untested until now. The two objectives target the diagnosed wall (runs/_probe_iter12_field.py: true cell top-1 only ~2% of 1024 despite landing in the top-20 33-58% of the time -- descriptor narrows things down but cannot finish separating lookalikes) very differently: contrastive only pushes different-location embeddings apart in the abstract, with no pressure to preserve WHAT the location looks like, so a lazy shortcut (e.g. collapsing to a few broad texture clusters) can drive the loss down while destroying exactly the fine layout detail that separates confusable lookalike districts. A reconstruction objective cannot take that shortcut: to repaint the clean daytime pixels of a 128x128 crop from an 8x8 feature map, the encoder is forced to keep genuine spatial/appearance detail (building outlines, road widths, texture layout) in every location's descriptor, not just a coarse identity code -- and because the target is always the SAME clean daytime reference regardless of which of the six lighting buckets the input came from, the pressure to canonicalize lighting comes for free as a side effect of the fidelity requirement, rather than being the entire (and, per 41/42, insufficient) point of the objective. This experiment isolates the pretext-task variable cleanly: architecture and training procedure otherwise mirror exp 42's already-implemented (and gate-checked) whole-pipeline shape (from-scratch conv trunk, lighting gate removed, unified field) so any metric difference is attributable to reconstruction vs. contrastive pretraining, not to incidental shape differences. Unlike exp 41/42's staged pretrain-then-finetune schedule, the reconstruction signal here runs as a joint auxiliary loss for the entire training run (library L1 flavor B) rather than a separate up-front phase, so the canonicalization pressure cannot be forgotten during fine-tuning the way a one-shot pretraining phase's benefit can fade.
method: Replace model.py's ImageNet-pretrained MobileNetV3-Small trunk with a from-scratch 4-stage conv encoder (32->64->96->64 channels, stride 16, 8x8x64 output). Add a small training-only transpose-conv decoder (mirrors the encoder: 64->96->64->32->3, sigmoid output) that reconstructs the clean daytime reference.tif crop from the encoder's own feature map -- discarded before ONNX export, so it costs nothing in the deployment budget. Every training step, in addition to the existing CE-field + coord-L2 + conf-BCE loss, add an L1 pixel-reconstruction loss (weight 1.0) between the decoder's output and the pixel-aligned reference.tif crop (same cx/cy/rotation angle as the input, extracted via the frozen pipeline.dataset.extract_crop) -- for every lighting bucket, every crop, every step, not a separate pretraining phase. The lighting gate (two-expert day/night blend) is removed and replaced with a single unified field head, since the reconstruction target is always the canonical daytime scene regardless of input lighting bucket, giving the trunk direct pressure to normalize lighting differences into one representation rather than needing a routing mechanism downstream. All parameters (encoder, field head, confidence head, decoder) train under one shared LR/schedule instead of the old pretrained-trunk 10x-slower discount, since nothing here is transferred.
expected_outcome: If descriptor fidelity (not merely lighting-invariance-in-the-abstract) is the missing ingredient contrastive pretraining failed to supply, worst-case median error improves from the champion's 743.07 m to roughly 550-700 m (6-26%), and should clear exp 42's own result (846.42 m) by a wide enough margin to distinguish 'reconstruction beats contrastive' from noise. Mechanism-level signature to check post-hoc: a matched-protocol rerun of the iter-12 field probe should show true-cell top-1 rate rise measurably above both the champion's ~2% baseline and whatever exp 42 measured (not logged, but assumed near-baseline given its regression), since fine-detail preservation should directly help separate lookalike cells that differ in sub-crop layout. Falsification forks: (a) if the metric regresses to exp-42-or-worse territory (>=800 m) with healthy reconstruction loss (visibly low pixel L1, e.g. <0.05 by the end of training), the descriptor-quality hypothesis itself is refuted for BOTH self-supervised pretext families tried so far -- the wall is not what the from-scratch trunk is taught to preserve/discriminate, redirecting firmly toward either a genuinely larger from-scratch capacity budget or abandoning the from-scratch-trunk family in favor of a different permissively-licensed PRETRAINED family (still legal under the backbone ban, since only the ORIGINAL family is banned, not pretraining itself); (b) if reconstruction loss stays high/flat (pixel L1 not dropping below ~0.15), the from-scratch encoder is too small or the 128x128->8x8->128x128 bottleneck is too lossy for this pretext task to ever succeed, arguing for a shallower stride (e.g. stride-8 bottleneck) rather than abandoning reconstruction; (c) if the metric improves but less than exp 42's own predicted range, both self-supervised objectives partially help and the remaining gap points at ensembling/longer joint training rather than either objective being wrong.
eli5: Instead of starting the drone's 'eye' with a pretrained network that learned to recognize everyday objects like dogs and cars (which never had to learn to tell one street from another), we make it learn a new party trick from scratch: given a photo taken at dawn, dusk, or pitch-black night, it has to repaint what that same spot looks like in clean daylight. To repaint convincingly it has to actually remember fine details -- building shapes, road layouts -- not just a vague impression, which is exactly the kind of detail needed to tell two similar-looking neighborhoods apart. We tried a similar from-scratch idea last round (a matching game instead of repainting) and it didn't help; repainting is a stricter test that shouldn't let the network cheat by getting lazy.

stages.name: Camera frame
stages.detail: 128×128 px aerial crop, one of 6 simulated lighting conditions

stages.name: Rotation fan
stages.detail: the crop is copied into its four 90° turns and all four travel through the same network side by side for the inference-time vote, same as before; new this round, the first (un-rotated) copy's own feature map is also handed off to the new training-only reconstruction decoder below, so this stage now feeds two different downstream consumers instead of one

stages.name: Feature extractor
stages.detail: a from-scratch, plain 4-stage conv stack (32->64->96->64 channels, no borrowed ImageNet weights, no MobileNetV3 family, no checkpoint file) trained under two simultaneous signals for the entire run: the usual location-scoring gradient, AND a pixel-reconstruction gradient that forces it to keep enough genuine appearance/layout detail to repaint the clean daytime look of the crop -- not just generic-object classification features the way the old pretrained trunk had

stages.name: Layout summary
stages.detail: same recipe as before -- a 1x1 conv squeezes the 8×8 feature grid into a spatial code, kept alongside the texture average -- resized for the new 64-channel trunk; because the trunk underneath now has to preserve real pixel-level structure to pass its reconstruction test, this code draws on a feature map trained for fidelity, not just classification-relevant texture

stages.name: Lighting gate
stages.detail: REMOVED: the old day/night switch and its two separate scorers are deleted. The reconstruction target is always the SAME clean daytime scene no matter which of the six lighting buckets the input came from, so the trunk is directly pushed to erase lighting differences into one canonical representation instead of needing a downstream router to compensate

stages.name: Probability map
stages.detail: a single unified scorer (no day/night blend) reads the layout summary directly into one heat map over the area

stages.name: Decode
stages.detail: the same contrast-sharpened balance-point decode as before, now reading the single unified heat map instead of a blended two-expert one -- the sharpening math is unchanged, but what it decodes is structurally simpler

stages.name: Confidence
stages.detail: reads the heat map's own shape as before (crisp hotspot vs. washed-out smear) plus, newly, the crop's own raw brightness directly, since that signal is no longer spent on routing between two scorers

stages.name: Training data
stages.detail: the existing confusability-weighted place sampler (exp 35) feeds the usual epochs unchanged; NEW: every crop drawn, in every lighting bucket, also loads its pixel-aligned twin from the untouched reference.tif raster (same cx/cy/rotation angle) as a repaint TARGET -- free, since the frozen relight pipeline renders every bucket from that same reference scene

stages.name: Training signal
stages.detail: the usual grading (CE against the true spot, coordinate L2, confidence BCE) PLUS a new pixel-reconstruction L1 loss (weight 1.0) between a small training-only decoder's repainted crop and the true clean daytime crop -- active every step of the whole run, not a separate up-front phase; the decoder is discarded before export and never flies

stages.name: Training schedule
stages.detail: no separate pretraining phase (unlike the reverted contrastive-pretrained attempt, exp 41/42) -- the reconstruction gradient runs inside the SAME single epoch loop from step one; because the trunk is from-scratch rather than transferred, every parameter (trunk, heads, and the new reconstruction decoder) now shares one learning-rate group instead of the old 10x trunk/head split reserved for gently fine-tuning pretrained features

implementation_brief:
model/model.py: (1) Delete the `from torchvision.models import mobilenet_v3_small` import, `PRETRAINED_TRUNK_PATH`, `IMAGENET_MEAN`/`IMAGENET_STD` buffers, and `_build_pretrained_trunk()`. (2) Add a from-scratch encoder module: 4 Conv2d+ReLU stages, kernel 3, stride 2, padding 1, channels 3->32->64->96->64 (128x128 -> 64x8x8). Assign it to `self.features` (same attribute name train.py already reads for the trunk/head LR split -- see train.py note below) so it plugs into the existing forward-pass call sites (`self.features(...)`). (3) Add a training-only decoder module `self.recon_decoder`: 4 ConvTranspose2d layers, kernel 4, stride 2, padding 1, channels 64->96->64->32->3 (8x8 -> 128x128), followed by `torch.sigmoid` so output is in [0,1] matching the frame's own normalization (no ImageNet mean/std buffers needed anymore -- feed raw x in [0,1] directly to features, dropping the old normalize-by-buffers step entirely). (4) Delete `dark_logits` and `gate` (the two-expert blend); `loc_logits` becomes the single field head, `Linear(feat_ch + layout_ch*fmap_hw*fmap_hw, grid_k*grid_k)` with feat_ch=64. (5) In `forward()`: keep the existing `_c4_stack` -> `self.features` -> field/decode/confidence path exactly as today's shape (just without the gate blend -- `logits4 = self.loc_logits(torch.cat([f4, layout4], dim=1))`, then the same C4-average, sharpened softmax, soft-argmax, and confidence-head-with-brightness as before). Add a NEW forward argument `return_recon: bool = False`; when True, additionally slice `fmap[:n]` (the un-rotated copy's feature map, since `_c4_stack` puts the original x in rows 0:n) through `self.recon_decoder` and return it as a third tuple element alongside `(out, logits)`. When `return_recon` is False (including the bare `model(x)` call `export_onnx` and `estimate_position` use), the decoder is never invoked, so it does not enter the traced ONNX graph and does not affect exported size -- verify this by confirming `export_onnx`'s call site still uses only `model(dummy)` with no extra args. (6) `loss_fn` gets two new optional parameters: `loss_fn(pred, logits, target_uv, recon_pred=None, recon_target=None)`. When both are given, add `torch.nn.functional.l1_loss(recon_pred, recon_target)` (no extra weight multiplier needed since it's already weight 1.0) to the returned total loss; when either is None (keeps the signature usable if ever called without recon, though train.py always supplies both), skip that term. (7) Rewrite the module docstring's history section in your own words describing this round's change (the docstring-stripping in autoresearch/backbonecheck.py already exempts prose from the identifier scan, so historical narration is not a compliance risk, but keep it accurate and don't literally name the banned backbone as if it were still in use). Preserve verbatim: train.py's CLI, and the ONNX export contract (1x3x128x128 float32 [0,1] in, [[u,v,conf]] out) stated in the module docstring's top paragraph.

model/train.py: (1) `sample_epoch`: also open `reference.tif` once per call (same rasterio pattern already used in `compute_cell_weights`/`prepare_realizations`), and for every crop appended to `xs`, also extract the reference crop at the SAME `cx, cy, angle` via `pipeline.dataset.extract_crop` and append to a parallel list `rs`; return `(x, y, r)` with `r` the stacked uint8 reference-crop tensor (same shape/dtype/normalization convention as `x`). (2) In `train_area`'s inner loop: unpack `x, y, r = sample_epoch(...)`; convert `rb = r[idx].to(device).permute(0,3,1,2).contiguous().float().div_(255.0)` alongside the existing `xb`/`yb`; call `out, logits, recon = model(xb, return_logits=True, return_recon=True)`; call `loss = loss_fn(out, logits, yb, recon_pred=recon, recon_target=rb)`. (3) Replace the trunk/head two-LR-group optimizer split with a single param group at `1e-3` for all `model.parameters()` (no more `trunk_params`/`head_params`/`trunk_ids` split -- the 10x discount existed only to protect pretrained features, which no longer exist). (4) Update the `info['init']` string to describe the new strategy, e.g. `'from-scratch:4-stage conv encoder + joint pixel-reconstruction auxiliary decoder (discarded before export)'`. Everything else in train.py (confusability-weighted `compute_cell_weights`, `prepare_realizations`, `calibrate_conf_shift`, the CLI, epoch/step counts, cosine schedule shape) is unchanged. Preserve exactly: the `python -m model.train --area ... --out-dir ... [--epochs N]` CLI contract, and that `calibrate_conf_shift` and `export_onnx` still receive a model whose bare `model(x)` call (no `return_recon`) yields the frozen `[u, v, conf]` output shape.
